name: OpenMark AI Router
description: Benchmark-driven model routing. Uses one isolated low-cost classification call, then lets the routed model produce the real reply on the same turn with benchmark-based model selection from OpenMark AI (https://openmark.ai). Captures your real default model for classifier/passthrough use, prefers direct provider keys before OpenRouter fallback when available, and includes a local dashboard for router health, providers, benchmark visibility, benchmark CSV import, category descriptions, and basic controls.

classifier_model.description: Optional override for the isolated classification call. Small cheap models are recommended. Leave empty to use your captured default model.

no_route_passthrough.description: Optional override for unrouted messages and short-message passthrough. Leave empty to use your captured default model.

routing_strategy.description: Benchmark ranking strategy used to choose the routed model.

port.description: Port for the embedded routing server

gateway_port.description: Port of the OpenClaw gateway (used for classification loopback). Default is 18789.

show_routing_card.description: Prepend routing card to responses when a task is routed

restore_delay_s.description: Seconds before timer-based restore returns the default model to openmark/auto when hook-based restore is unavailable.

fallback_count.description: How many ranked fallback models to include with the routed reply run.

cost_ceiling.description: Optional max cost per run used by the best_under_budget strategy.

latency_ceiling_s.description: Optional max latency in seconds used by the best_under_latency strategy.
